xfail.msMeta/Particles_w3c.xml/particlesS002: 1.0-vs-1.1 particle-restriction tradeoff: helium's order-independent recurseAll accepts an order-permuted xs:all restriction that strict XSD 1.0 order-preserving Recurse rejects but XSD 1.1 abolished (MHK: invalid in 1.0, valid in 1.1); the reorder is language-identical (xs:all is unordered) and helium matches the semantic subset / libxml2 leniency; passes in the XSD 1.1 suite
xfail.msMeta/Particles_w3c.xml/particlesT002: 1.0-vs-1.1 particle-restriction tradeoff: order-independent recurseChoiceUnordered accepts a reordered xs:choice restriction that strict XSD 1.0 RecurseLax rejects but 1.1 abolished; language-identical; passes in the 1.1 suite
xfail.msMeta/Particles_w3c.xml/particlesT009: 1.0-vs-1.1 particle-restriction tradeoff: same order-independent recurseChoiceUnordered class as particlesT002 over an unbounded reordered choice; passes in the 1.1 suite
xfail.msMeta/Particles_w3c.xml/particlesZ023: 1.0-vs-1.1 particle-restriction tradeoff: single-branch choice narrowing accepted after pointless-particle/lax rules that XSD 1.0 rejects but 1.1 permits; language subset; passes in the 1.1 suite
xfail.msMeta/Particles_w3c.xml/particlesZ024: 1.0-vs-1.1 particle-restriction tradeoff: same choice-narrowing-via-group-refs class as particlesZ023; passes in the 1.1 suite
xfail.msMeta/Group_w3c.xml/groupH021v: 1.0-vs-1.1 particle-restriction tradeoff: choice-branch drop with group refs (order/pointless leniency) accepted; XSD 1.0 rejects, 1.1 permits; semantically a plain choice narrowing; passes in the 1.1 suite
xfail.msMeta/Particles_w3c.xml/particlesZ001: the XSD 1.0 §3.9.6 RecurseAsIfGroup rule is manifest-declared ambiguous; the element{0,unbounded}:choice{0,unbounded}-containing-wildcard subsumption is only provable via the XSD 1.1 particle-language-subset fallback, which helium confines to the 1.1 path (1.0-byte-identical mandate)
xfail.msMeta/Particles_w3c.xml/particlesW006: particle-restriction subsumption of an empty-language (maxOccurs=0) base/derived model; W3C bugzilla 4952 (status queried)
xfail.msMeta/SimpleType_w3c.xml/ste110: circular union (member chain st -> st2 -> st) is correctly rejected per cos-no-circular-unions; helium matches libxml2 (rejects circular type definitions); the expected-valid outcome is disputed (W3C bug 4957, queried)
xfail.msMeta/Schema_w3c.xml/schG6: two <xs:import namespace='ns-b'> with different schemaLocations; helium and libxml2 load only the first location, so the cross-document duplicate component is not detected (libxml2-parity, verbatim 'namespace already imported' warning); disputed (W3C bug 4133, queried)
xfail.msMeta/Schema_w3c.xml/schG11: cross-document duplicate {ns-a} component (declared in A and reached again via B's import of C) is not detected by the if-not-exists component merge (libxml2-parity import handling); disputed (W3C bug 4133, queried)
xfail.msMeta/Schema_w3c.xml/schG12: the instance's {ns-b} element is unresolvable under the strict wildcard because the second <xs:import namespace='ns-b'> location is skipped (libxml2-parity same-namespace import dedup, same root as schG6/schG11); disputed (W3C bug 4133, queried)
xfail.msMeta/Schema_w3c.xml/schU1: mutual/circular <xs:redefine> (A redefines B, B redefines A) is rejected as a duplicate component; the test is explicitly marked a disputed-test (W3C bug 4135)
xfail.msMeta/DataTypes_w3c.xml/anyURI_a004_133: the enumerated xs:anyURI values (ftp/gopher/mailto/news/telnet URIs) are accepted by the maximally-permissive post-errata xs:anyURI value space; the expected-invalid instance outcome is disputed (W3C bug 4126, queried)
xfail.msMeta/Additional_w3c.xml/addB188: requires user-supplied-schema-for-the-xml-namespace support (validating xml:* attributes against an imported schema for http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace); XSD 1.0 treats xml:* attributes as always-allowed; disputed (w3c/xsdtests#11, queried)
xfail.msMeta/Attribute_w3c.xml/attP031: the MS test asserts valid per MSXML leniency, but per XSD 1.0 section 3.4.2 a use='prohibited' attribute creates no {attribute use}, so the attribute present in the instance makes it invalid (cvc-complex-type.3.2.1); helium matches libxml2 (rejects)
